response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend engineer with over 15 years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The two hardest requirements — native-level Japanese proficiency and deep C/Linux kernel experience for embedded networking security — are both absent. His language skills are English, Romanian, and basic German; Japanese is not listed at all. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented tooling, with only peripheral C/C++ exposure from a single 6-month role at National Instruments. He has no demonstrated experience in L2/L3 networking, LAN switch development, MACsec, 802.1X, embedded firmware, or any security domain relevant to this position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Demonstrated C/C++ usage at National Instruments and in personal projects (Sound Position Server using Linux/Jack/C++)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that transfers to any engineering culture
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — native-level Japanese is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 embedded networking stacks
No Linux kernel development or source-level modification experience; Linux use is limited to DevOps/infrastructure context
